content:
**Sam Soverel Triumphs at 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship**

*Las Vegas, NV — December 14, 2025*

Sam Soverel has been crowned champion of the 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Championship, outlasting a star-studded 64-player field in a dramatic finale broadcast on Peacock. The event, returning for the first time in over a decade, saw Soverel defeat seasoned pros and online phenoms alike, ultimately besting Jason Koon in a nail-biting final match.

“This feels incredible,” Soverel said moments after clinching the title and the $500,000 first prize. “Every round was a battle, but I just tried to stay focused on each hand, each decision. I’ve watched this tournament for years—I never imagined I’d win it.”

Soverel’s path to victory included high-profile wins against Michael Mizrachi in the quarterfinals and Liv Boeree in the round of sixteen. Notably, former NFL star Richard Seymour and WSOP regular Esther Taylor were both eliminated in earlier rounds.

Poker analyst Maria Ho commented, “Sam’s adaptability really set him apart. Heads-up play is about reading your opponent and shifting gears—he did that flawlessly.”

The championship’s return was met with rave reviews, drawing both die-hard fans and a new generation of players. With his win, Soverel cements his place in poker history as the 2025 National Heads-Up Poker Champion.
